steps.observed: Login form displayed with email, password, company code fields

steps.action: Fill email: validation.admin@example.com, password: Validation1!..., company code: MOCK

assertions.title: Login with Invalid Credentials Shows Error

steps.action: Fill email: validation.admin@example.com, wrong password, company code: MOCK

steps.expected: Inline error message shown, stay on /login
steps.observed: Toast error: 'Invalid email, password, or company code.' displayed, stayed on /login

assertions.title: Login with Wrong Company Code Shows Error

steps.action: Fill valid email/password, company code: WRONG

steps.observed: Toast error: 'Company not found: WRONG' displayed, stayed on /login

evidence.network: Error response returned with company-specific message

assertions.title: MFA Redirect After MFA-Enabled Login

steps.action: Attempted login with all test accounts (admin, superadmin)
steps.observed: No test account has MFA enabled - all logins proceed directly to portal

assertions.issues: No MFA-enabled test account available in seed data. Tested admin (MOCK) and superadmin (SKE) - both bypass MFA. Frontend code has MFA redirect logic (LoginPage.tsx routes to /mfa when requiresMfa=true), but no test account triggers this flow.

assertions.title: MFA Verification Completes Login
assertions.issues: Blocked by same issue as VAL-AUTH-004: no MFA-enabled test account with known TOTP secret. Frontend has MfaPage.tsx and verifyMfa() in authApi, but cannot test end-to-end without a provisioned account.

assertions.title: Forced Password Change Gate

steps.action: Searched for test account with mustChangePassword=true
steps.observed: No test account has mustChangePassword=true flag set

assertions.issues: No test account with mustChangePassword=true exists in seed data. Frontend code has the redirect logic (LoginPage.tsx navigates to /change-password when mustChangePassword is true), but cannot test without a provisioned account.

steps.observed: Form with 'Work email' field and 'Send reset link' button displayed
Confirmation: 'Check your email - If an account with that address exists, we've sent a link to reset your password.'

assertions.title: Password Reset via Token

steps.observed: Email received in MailHog at localhost:8025 with token
Form with 'New password', 'Confirm password', and 'Reset password' button displayed
Password reset succeeded, redirected to /superadmin (auto-logged in after reset)
Reset form renders even with invalid token (validation on submit)

evidence.network: Password reset token extracted from MailHog email

assertions.issues: Minor: After successful password reset, the app redirects to the user's portal (/superadmin) instead of /login. The user is automatically authenticated after reset. This differs from the contract's expected redirect to /login, but may be by design for better UX.

frictions.description: Login page sometimes renders with no interactive elements initially — requires a 2-second wait after networkidle for the form to appear
frictions.resolution: Added wait 2000 after networkidle before snapshot

frictions.description: Password reset token extraction required MailHog API access (localhost:8025) — not documented in user-testing.md
frictions.resolution: Used curl to query MailHog API and extract token from email body via regex

blockers.description: No MFA-enabled test account available — cannot test VAL-AUTH-004 and VAL-AUTH-005
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tested login with all available test accounts (admin, superadmin) — none triggered MFA redirect

blockers.description: No test account with mustChangePassword=true — cannot test VAL-AUTH-006
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Searched for existing accounts with this flag — none found

summary: Tested 8 assertions: 5 passed, 3 blocked (VAL-AUTH-004, VAL-AUTH-005, VAL-AUTH-006 — no MFA-enabled or mustChangePassword test accounts available). Login flow works correctly for valid/invalid credentials and wrong company codes. Forgot-password flow shows non-enumerating confirmation. Password reset works but redirects to portal instead of /login.
